meta.audit_title: Agentic Commerce Readiness Audit — Beauty Edition 1

score.description: Crawlability and basic infrastructure work; structured data emission is the primary gap for agent retrieval.

criteria.title: AI crawler access
criteria.one_liner: All 8 tested AI bots allowed in robots.txt.
criteria.log_summary: AI crawler access. robots.txt allows all 8 tested bots (GPTBot / PerplexityBot / ClaudeBot / anthropic-ai / Google-Extended / Amazonbot / CCBot / FacebookBot).

criteria.title: PDP server-side render
criteria.one_liner: Only 2/3 sampled PDPs render all core elements in raw HTML.
criteria.log_summary: PDP server-side render. 2/3 sampled PDPs return complete product data without JS. Others missing one or more of title/price/description.

criteria.title: Sitemap freshness
criteria.one_liner: Sitemap has 13 URLs with fresh <lastmod> timestamps.
criteria.log_summary: Sitemap freshness. Valid sitemap with 13 recently-updated URLs (most recent: 2026-04-03T17:00:46-04:00).

criteria.title: Product schema
criteria.log_summary: Product schema. 1/3 PDPs ship complete Product JSON-LD. Others missing required fields or the block entirely.

criteria.title: AggregateRating
criteria.one_liner: 2/3 PDPs ship Review or AggregateRating schema.
criteria.log_summary: AggregateRating. 2/3 PDPs carry rating schema. Reviews likely displayed visually but not exposed as structured data on others.

criteria.title: FAQPage / HowTo schema
criteria.one_liner: FAQPage/HowTo schema found on 1 checked page(s).
criteria.log_summary: FAQPage / HowTo schema. Found on 1 page(s) — homepage or help/how-to surface.

criteria.title: PDP titles
criteria.one_liner: 4/5 sampled titles include use-case or differentiator cues.
criteria.log_summary: PDP titles. 4/5 sampled titles include use-case specifics (fabric, fit, target audience).

criteria.title: Description depth
criteria.log_summary: PDP descriptions. 0/5 sampled descriptions cover 3+ buyer-context cues. Others are spec-heavy with limited context.

fields.label: Checked
fields.content: 3 sampled PDPs via raw HTML fetch

fields.label: Found
fields.content: 1 of 3 PDPs don't render all core fields in raw HTML. Missing: title. Examples: filling-good-trial-size.

fields.label: Fix
fields.content: Audit the product-template rendering path to confirm title/price/description appear in raw HTML without JS. Agents cannot execute JavaScript on merchant sites.

fields.label: Checked
fields.content: 3 sampled PDPs — raw HTML via source-level fetch

fields.label: Found
fields.content: Only 1/3 PDPs ship complete Product JSON-LD with all commonly-recommended fields (name, image, brand, offers.price, offers.availability, and an identifier).

fields.label: Fix
fields.content: Ensure every PDP template emits Product JSON-LD with the Google rich-results minimum fields. See Google's product structured-data docs.

fields.label: Found
fields.content: 2/3 PDPs expose rating data as JSON-LD with real ratingValue and reviewCount. Others likely display reviews visually but don't emit structured data — typically a widget toggle that hasn't been enabled.

fields.label: Fix
fields.content: Enable review-schema output in your review plugin (Yotpo, Judge.me, Okendo, etc.) — often a single setting. See schema.org/AggregateRating.

fields.label: Found
fields.content: Organization schema missing or incomplete. Missing: name, url, sameAs. sameAs should link to at least 2 social profiles for entity recognition.

fields.label: Fix
fields.content: Add (or extend) Organization JSON-LD in the site-wide <head>: name, url, logo, and a sameAs array of verified social-profile URLs. Low risk, high leverage for AI entity recognition.

fields.label: Found
fields.content: 0/5 descriptions cover 3+ of: target skin/fit type, comparison to similar products, ingredient/material rationale, sizing reasoning, usage guidance, counter-indications. Most are spec-heavy with limited buyer context.

fields.label: Caveat
fields.content: Evaluation via rendered-response text. Descriptions may include buyer-context content in expandable sections or tabs that weren't surfaced.

fields.label: Fix
fields.content: Add a structured "Why you'll love it" block to every PDP — who it's for, how it compares, when to use, what it isn't for. At catalog scale this is a content pipeline (LLM-assisted generation from existing spec sheets is plausible).

fixes.title: Ship complete Product JSON-LD on every PDP
fixes.description: Highest-leverage structured-data change. Ensures AI agents can extract name, price, availability, and identifiers cleanly. Lightweight if product data already exists server-side.

fixes.title: Enable AggregateRating output in your review plugin
fixes.description: Usually a single setting in Yotpo / Judge.me / Okendo that exposes existing review data as JSON-LD. Reviews already live on the site — this just makes them machine-readable.

fixes.title: Add complete Organization JSON-LD with sameAs
fixes.description: 10-line site-wide <head> addition — name, url, logo, and links to verified social profiles. Low risk, foundational for AI entity recognition.

real_story.heading: The real story
paragraphs: Farmacy Beauty is around the category median — not yet agent-ready, but not structurally behind.
A 4/9 score sits in the middle of premium DTC beauty. Implementing 2-3 targeted fixes would lift this brand into the top tier.

disclosure: Part of the Nohi Agentic Commerce Readiness Index 2026 — Edition 1 (Premium DTC Beauty, 40 brands). Scoring via the score_brand.py audit tool on 2026-04-18. Findings are sample-based (3 PDPs / 5 titles). Does not audit uniqueness (C10, deferred) or GEO (geographic/local retrieval). Point-in-time: brands may have changed since the scoring date. Full methodology.
